sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 14.33 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.14)

statement.latex: Does there exist a finitely presented pro-$p$-group ($p$being a prime) which contains an isomorphic copy of every countably based pro-$p$-group? { R.\,I.\,Grigorchuk} Yes, there does: the Nottingham group over${ F} _p$for$p>2$, which was shown to be finitely presented in (M.\,V.\,Ershov, J. London Math. Soc., 71 (2005), 362--378). (M.\,Ershov, Letter of 19.10.2009.)

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory IV_PROBLEM_UNDEFINED; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
